primary_diagnosis.name: Reactive arthritis (post-Campylobacter triad) — septic/gonococcal joint and uveitis to be excluded

pathophysiology_summary: Reactive arthritis is a sterile, immune-mediated arthritis triggered ~1–4 weeks after an enteric (e.g. Campylobacter) or genitourinary infection, classically an asymmetric lower-limb oligoarthritis with enthesitis, plus extra-articular features — conjunctivitis and urethritis (the reactive triad). This mimics disseminated gonococcal infection and septic arthritis. Reassuring features are the temporal link to a documented gut infection, multi-system involvement, non-purulent conjunctivitis and no necrotic pustules/fiery tense joint/high fever. However, a warm effused joint is a 'hot joint' that must be aspirated to exclude septic/gonococcal arthritis; reactive arthritis is also associated with sight-threatening acute anterior uveitis; and urethritis warrants STI screening. Telehealth cannot examine/aspirate the joint, perform a slit-lamp eye assessment, or take urinary/STI samples, so the correct action is urgent in-person assessment with ankle aspiration (± synovial analysis) and STI screening — not a remote injection/NSAID as a substitute — with emergency care for a fiery hot joint/high fever/rigors or any sudden eye pain/vision change.

differential.evidence_basis: Screen for necrotic pustules, sexual exposure; STI testing.
A warm effused joint must be aspirated to exclude infection.
Extra-articular features and enthesitis point away from crystals; aspiration would settle it.
Triad with no fiery/tense joint or rigors — but the hot joint still needs aspiration and STI screening.
Lower probability but not excludable without STI testing/synovial analysis.
Extra-articular triad and enthesitis; crystals would confirm/exclude on aspiration.

differential.diagnosis: Reactive arthritis — septic/gonococcal excluded by aspiration/STI screen
differential.evidence_basis: Working telehealth position: likely reactive arthritis, but urgent in-person assessment with ankle aspiration and STI screening (and eye safety-netting) is required before this is accepted.

red_flags.description: A warm, fluid-filled ('hot') ankle joint — must be aspirated to exclude septic/gonococcal arthritis
red_flags.clinical_significance: Septic/gonococcal arthritis rapidly destroys joints and can disseminate; a hot joint requires aspiration.

red_flags.description: Ankle becoming fiery red/tense/unbearably hot, or high fever with rigors/necrotic skin pustules
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would strongly indicate septic/disseminated gonococcal infection needing emergency care.

red_flags.description: Sudden eye pain, photophobia, or vision blurring/loss (acute anterior uveitis)

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Attributing the warm ankle to reactive arthritis without aspirating to exclude septic/gonococcal arthritis.
Not safety-netting sight-threatening anterior uveitis (assuming all eye redness is benign conjunctivitis).
Prescribing IM/high-dose NSAIDs remotely instead of arranging joint assessment/aspiration and STI screening.
Not arranging gonorrhoea/chlamydia screening for urethritis.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Warm effused ankle with low-grade fever; no fiery/tense joint or rigors; no eye pain/vision change — aspiration and STI screen required
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports urgent in-person assessment with aspiration and STI screening, and eye safety-netting.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Joint examination and aspiration/synovial fluid analysis
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Arrange urgent in-person assessment with ankle aspiration (± synovial analysis) and STI screening; safety-net the eye; escalate to ED for a fiery/tense hot joint, high fever/rigors, or sudden eye pain/vision change.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Managing a warm effused joint remotely as reactive arthritis could miss septic/gonococcal arthritis; missing uveitis risks vision.

guidelines_referenced.reference: Therapeutic Guidelines: Rheumatology (acute hot joint/reactive arthritis)
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: An acutely warm swollen joint requires aspiration to exclude septic arthritis; reactive arthritis is treated with NSAIDs after infection is excluded.
Urethritis warrants NAAT screening for gonorrhoea and chlamydia; treat and contact-trace as indicated.
